title: Money vs Time
context: Life priorities, financial behavior, survival pressure, and personal decision-making
surface_question: Which matters more, money or time?
a_causal_chain: value storage -> exchange -> access -> options -> protection -> leverage
b_causal_chain: existence -> attention -> action -> experience -> relationship -> life
hidden_difference: Money can be regained. Time can only be spent.
a_failure_mode: Money fails when it becomes the goal instead of a tool, or when trust/value collapses.
b_failure_mode: Time fails when it is unstructured, wasted, or consumed without meaningful direction.
conditional_verdict: Money protects time when survival is unstable. Time becomes the higher-order value when survival is stable.
final_line: Money controls options. Time contains life.
